missing-values-during-insert.label: Missing values during insert
missing-values-during-insert.summary: Learn how SQL handles missing values during INSERT, including the use of NULL and DEFAULT values in the inventory_items table.

sketch2.title: What Happens If You Omit a NOT NULL Column?

multi-1.title: Which columns can be omitted in an INSERT?
multi-1.prompt: Which of the following columns in the inventory_items table can be omitted from an INSERT statement and still allow the row to be inserted successfully? Select all that apply.
multi-1.hint: Look for columns that have a DEFAULT value or allow NULL.

help.concept: Columns with a DEFAULT value or that allow NULL can be omitted from an INSERT statement.
help.hint_1: Check the schema for columns with DEFAULT or NULL allowed.
help.hint_2: Omit columns like notes, status, is_test, and last_updated if they have defaults or allow NULL.

fill-1.title: Default value for status
fill-1.prompt: What value will the status column have if you omit it in an INSERT statement for inventory_items?
fill-1.hint: Check the DEFAULT value for status in the table schema.

help.concept: If a column has a DEFAULT value and is omitted in an INSERT, SQL uses the DEFAULT.
help.hint_1: Look at the CREATE TABLE statement for the default value of status.
help.hint_2: The default for status is a string set in the schema.

fill-1.template: If you omit the status column in an INSERT, its value will be: ___

sketch-1.title: Handling Missing Values in INSERT
sketch-1.bodyMarkdown:
When inserting data into a table like `inventory_items`, you don't always have to provide a value for every column. If a column allows NULL or has a DEFAULT value, you can omit it in your INSERT statement. For example:

```sql
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price) VALUES ('Desk Mat', 'Accessories', 19.99);
```

Here, columns like `status`, `is_test`, and `last_updated` will use their DEFAULT values, and `notes` will be set to NULL (since it allows NULLs).

sketch-2.title: Explicit NULL vs. Omitted Columns
sketch-2.bodyMarkdown:
You can explicitly insert NULL into a column that allows it:

```sql
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, notes) VALUES ('Sample Coaster', 'Kitchen', 3.50, NULL);
```

Or, you can simply omit the column from your column list, and SQL will use the DEFAULT value (if defined) or NULL (if allowed).

sketch-3.title: What Happens If You Omit a NOT NULL Column?
sketch-3.bodyMarkdown: If you try to insert a row without providing a value for a NOT NULL column that has no DEFAULT, SQL will return an error. Always check your table schema to know which columns require values and which can be omitted.
